title: The Mall Mole
location_hint: Shopping mall or adjacent retail area, indoor or covered outdoor environment
theme: A junior KGB operative must locate a mole hiding secret documents somewhere inside a bustling shopping mall

content_brief.tone_notes: KGB spy-agency dossier style — officiously bureaucratic but warm, funny, and kid-friendly throughout. The mall setting should feel like an exciting covert backdrop, not a mundane one. Think 'your mission, should you choose to accept it... is to find the pretzel stand first.' Difficulty and terrain were not specified by the placer; both defaulted to 2.
must_include: Field Agent Asad is introduced as the handler who planted the cache and is watching from a safe distance (possibly the food court)
The shopping mall setting is woven into the mission narrative — shops, shoppers, and mall geography all play a role
A dossier-style opening appropriate for young agents (e.g., CLASSIFIED, MISSION BRIEFING, EYES ONLY)
A cover story the finder can use if spotted — e.g., pretending to look for a lost balloon or compare sneakers
A warm congratulatory sign-off from Agent Asad acknowledging the finder as a newly certified KGB Mall Operative
must_avoid: Real names of specific mall chains, store brands, or exact hiding locations
Scary, violent, or dark spy content — must stay warm and age-appropriate
Adult-coded spy tropes (weapons, danger, betrayal) — keep stakes lighthearted and silly
Content that could alarm mall security or bystanders if read aloud
